_protocol: Phase 3.5-PRAGMATIC: 5+red+5 fetches with adversarial gap review COMPLETED

extracted.stabilization_status: Pricing unchanged Feb to Apr 2026 — stabilized at elevated levels

extracted._implication: Capacity expansion exists but AI demand absorbing it; consumer GPU allocation continues to be squeezed

extracted.2027_outlook: GOOGL up to $250B; not yet peaked
extracted._implication: t2_hyperscaler_capex_yoy_negative not firing; t2_hyperscaler_capex_above_25_3of4 firing strongly

extracted.amd_growth_trajectory: Approximately doubled in <1 year — meaningful momentum
extracted._implication: Alt-accelerator share approaching threshold (15% by Q4 2028 indicator); on trajectory to fire
NVIDIA gross margin still well above 55-65% band; t2_nvidia_gross_margin_compresses NOT firing; pricing power intact

gaps_identified: Used GPU 5-7yr post-peak: 1080 Ti $150-200 vs $700 MSRP — strong analog for H3/H4 by 2030
HBM/DRAM supply inflection 2027-Q4 earliest; oversupply scenario in 2027-28 plausible
AMD MI500 (2027) + Intel roadmap supports alt-accelerator share growth toward 15%
DeepSeek V4 73% per-token FLOPs reduction Q1 2026 — major inference efficiency breakthrough
Q1 2026 DRAM contract +55-60% QoQ — sustained tightness through 2027 H1

reference_class.definition: Consumer GPU pricing cycles 2010-2025 (mining boom, COVID, AI shortage); secondary: prior tech-component pricing cycles under demand-driven shortages

analog_events.name: Crypto mining boom 2017-18
analog_events.pattern: Premium of 50-200% above MSRP for 18 months, then sharp normalization within 6 months

analog_events.name: COVID GPU shortage 2020-22
analog_events.pattern: Premium of 100-300% for 24+ months, normalized as crypto demand collapsed + supply caught up

analog_events.name: AI shortage 2023-current
analog_events.pattern: Premium 5-30% sustained 18+ months; memory bottleneck binding through 2027 minimum

topic_priors.rationale: Round 2 anchored H3/H4 trajectories with: (a) 1080 Ti precedent showing real-price decline 5-7yr post-peak (analog for H3/H4); (b) Memory supply inflection 2027-2028; (c) AMD/Intel roadmap supports alt-accelerator threshold. Net: H1 down 10pp (current premium less likely to sustain to 2030), H2 up 2pp, H3 up 5pp, H4 up 3pp. By 2030: H3 modal as memory glut + alt-accelerators + efficiency gains compound.

extracted._implication: Prior shortage cycle GPUs (1080 Ti = 2017 launch) DID see substantial real price decline 5-7 years out — supports H3/H4 trajectories
Supply cycle inflection 2027-2028 supports H2/H3 trajectories by 2030; bimodal outcome distribution
Strong AMD MI400/500 roadmap supports alt-accelerator share growth toward 15% threshold by 2028; H2/H3 trajectory plausible
Major inference efficiency breakthrough Q1-Q2 2026 firing t3_inference_efficiency_breakthrough indicator; supports H3/H4 if cumulative efficiency gains compound
Through 2027 H1: continued tightness/premium pricing supports H1 short-term; relief 2028+ supports H2/H3 by 2030
